mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in residential construction permits (RSAFS) signals strong housing demand, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to improved consumer confidence and business investment.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of RSAFS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 9.5% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in UNRATE spikes exceed 5% of instances with negative signal or Rank IC remains above 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust economic activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate margins and capital expenditure.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in interaction periods show negative correlation (Rank IC < -0.05) or individual series effects dominate over joint behavior.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days due to higher discount rates and reduced equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in tightening periods show positive correlation (Rank IC > 0.1) or FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL interactions fail to predict directional moves.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average indicates weakening supply-side capacity, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days due to reduced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in INDPRO declines exceed 5% of instances with negative signal or Rank IC remains above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In low-unemployment regimes, rising payrolls (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics, which predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through improved consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in interaction periods show negative correlation (Rank IC < -0.05) or individual series effects dominate over joint behavior.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates expanding demand-side momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue and investment sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in interaction periods show negative correlation (Rank IC < -0.05) or individual series effects dominate over joint behavior.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 0.5% predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as accommodative monetary policy lowers borrowing costs and stimulates equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in easing periods show negative correlation (Rank IC < -0.05) or FEDFUNDS declines fail to predict directional moves.
